response: The internet traces back to 1960s U.S. Department of Defense networking research, with ARPANET demonstrated in 1969 via a message from UCLA to Stanford Research Institute. It gradually expanded through the 1970s, and in 1983 adoption of TCP/IP enabled today’s internet architecture, while Tim Berners-Lee created the World Wide Web in 1989 (with HTML, URLs, and HTTP); the Mosaic browser launched in 1993 to make the web widely usable. After the late-1990s dot-com boom and the 2001 crash, surviving companies such as Amazon and Google became major successes, and the mid-2000s rise of social media plus the iPhone (2007) drove mobile web adoption. Cloud computing accelerated with AWS in 2006, and today the internet connects over 5 billion people and supports nearly all aspects of modern life.
